calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg, via ZOOM, counsel for David Levin, Administrator; Tom Huster, via ZOOM, counsel for Respondents/Intestate Heirs.

The court at the outset queries Ms. Friedeberg regarding whether she has anything to add. Ms. Friedeberg does not and submits on the papers. The court then queries counsel regarding an expected appearance by Jerry Ku. Mr. Huster reports that he does not believe that Mr. Ku will be appearing and hopes that the court will order distribution, soon.

After additional discussion, the First Amended Accounting is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Continued To Jun-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Continued To Apr-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for David Levin (Administrator).
Robert Cross appeared by zoom for and with Jerry Ku (Beneficiary) by zoom.

The Court questions whether Ms. Friedeberg has invoices to support the amounts of costs advanced shown in the accounting. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the invoices were posted this morning, that the courtcall cost for a hearing of July 30, 2020 is a typo and it should be for a June 30th hearing, and that the bank statements were also e-filed. Mr. Cross stated that he has no objections to the fees or the lien, but there is a discrepancy in the amount of the reimbursement to Jerry Ku. The Court stated that it is not clear why any discrepancy that may exist can't be resolved between the owners at distribution. Ms. Friedeberg stated they are a nuetral party and they are trying to assist the parties. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to April 27, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to discuss the amount of Jerry Ku's reimbursement.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Continued To Mar-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for David Levin (Administrator).
Robert Cross appeared by zoom for Jerry Ku (Beneficiary).

The Court noted that it does not have the amended accounting that addresses the request for extraordinary fees, that the matter keeps being pushed out further and further for filing of an amended accounting, and that it keeps taking up a spot on the Court's calendar. Ms. Friedeberg explained that it's an accounting of a co-owned share of income and properties, that there are rents, property taxes, insurance, etc. that had to be reconciled and that money is owed to Mr. Ku. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that the co-owners are requesting to view the accounting, and requested a three-week continuance to provide the accounting to the other co-owners as well as Counsel. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to March 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Continued To Feb-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for David Levin (Administrator).
Robert Cross appeared by zoom for Jerry Ku.
Tom Huster appeared by zoom for Raymond Chan and other Heirs.

The Court questions why there is no amendment regarding the extraordinary fees. Ms. Friedeberg apologized to the Court for not filing the amendment, stated that there is an issue with the accounting that will require an amendment, that they need to make sure that all of the contributions from the co-owners are accounted for and received, and that they just received the bank statements yesterday. Ms. Friedeberg also requested a three to four-week continuance, stated that the parties are okay with it, that the fees were based on a very old value of the estate and that it does not reflect what her Client had to do. Mr. Chan and Mr. Huster stated that they are okay with allowing more time. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator and Petition for Its Settlement, etc., is continued to February 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Continued To Jul-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by telephone for David Levin (Administrator).
Robert Cross appeared by telephone for Jerry Ku.
Thomas Huster for Intestate Heirs.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that she hoped to have her petition filed today but was unable to do so and that she will have the petition filed this week. Status on close of estate is continued to July 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Reyes. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jun-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Continued To Apr-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for David Levin (Administrator).
Robert Cross for Jerry Ku.
Thomas Huster for the Intestate Heirs (except for Jerry Ku).

The Court stated that it is still looking for a petition to close the estate and an accounting from Jerry Ku. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the civil litigation was just recently completed, that the next step is to list the property for sale and that they will talk after the hearing to see if they can agree on a listing agent. Mr. Cross stated that there are some construction and permit issues that need to be resolved before they can sell the property, requested a three month continuance to deal with those issues and give them a chance to work on getting a listing agent, stated that he is not sure where Mr. Ku's accounting stands and that he believes Mr. Ku hired a Professional to assist him with the accounting. Mr. Huster agrees with Counsel and asked to have the matter continued to April. The Court stated that the accounting was ordered on April 27, 2018 and that it needs to happen. Status on close of estate is continued to April 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court is requiring that the accounting petition be filed prior April 1st. The Court informed Ms. Friedeberg that if there is no petition to close the estate, there needs to be a petition for approval of a report on status of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for David Levin (Current Administrator).
Christian Foote for Jerry Ku.

Mr. Foote stated that he thinks the probate matter should be stayed until the Civil Court makes a determination as to who the property belongs to and that the trial is set for May 6, 2019. Ms. Friedeberg stated that there is real property with tenants in it, that rents need to be collected and that repairs need to be made to address the Tenant's concerns. Mr. Foote stated that he does not object to continued administration by Mr. Levin, and that there is a Harry and a Harold that live at the same address. The Court stated that it will treat this as a request to stay the action. Mr. Foote will provide an order staying this proceeding. The Court granted the status report (subject to receipt and review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Dec-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christian Foote for Jerry Ku.
Trisha Friedeberg appeared by telephone for David Levin (Administrator).

Ms. Foote stated that he has courtesy copies of the status report and the notice of hearing (copies provided in Court), that the other intestate heirs are represented by Mr. Huster and that he has not provided notice to the individual heirs. The Court explained to Mr. Foote that he needs to give notice to all of the interested parties, not just to the Attorneys. The Status Report is continued to December 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference
Off Calendar Jan-08-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David King for Jerry Ku (Respondent).
Thomas Huster for Ana Wong (Moving Party).

Mr. King stated that the appeal is still pending, that no briefing schedule has been set by the Court of Appeal, that they are still waiting and that his position is this matter should be stayed. Mr. King also stated that they were unable to reach resolution and requested a continuance of the January 19, 2018 evidentiary hearing. Mr. Huster stated that he wants to proceed with the January 19th hearing. The Court stated that it will not continue the hearing as it was set by Judge Stewart. The status conference is off calendar.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Vacate Fraudulently Obtained Court Orders, And For Related Relief
Off Calendar Nov-20-2017 Continued To Jan-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Huster for Intestate Heirs.
David King for Terry Ku (Respondent).

Counsel reported that they did not get very far with their meet and confer, provided history, and spoke of the appeal, the quiet title action and the stipulation. Mr. Huster requested a hearing on this petition stating that the appeal does not have any effect on it. Mr. King argued that it does not make any sense to go forward on this until the appeal is decided. The Court set a status conference for January 8, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Each Counsel are to submit a short status statement regarding the other cases by January 5, 2018. The Petition to Vacate Fraudulently Obtained Court Orders, etc. is continued to January 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for an evidentiary hearing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Z. Grimaldi. Not reported.

Petition To Vacate Fraudulently Obtained Court Orders, And For Related Relief
Off Calendar Oct-23-2017 Continued To Nov-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Huster for Petitioners.
David King for and with Jerry Ku.

Mr. King provided background history and explained circumstances with respect to how title was held and changed from the time the property was purchased. Counsel spoke of their positions with respect to whether or not the appeal stays the proceedings that are filed in Probate Court or the Quiet Title action that is filed in the Civil Court. Discussion was held regarding setting aside of the order for final distribution, the probate matter starting back at square one, and appointment of a neutral or of another family member for joint administration. Mr. King raised the issue of dismissing the appeal provided Mr. Huster agrees to an evidentiary hearing and or trial on both theories (adverse possession/principal of equity to give effect on the intent of the three Brothers). The Petition to Vacate Fraudulently Obtained Court Orders, etc. is continued to November 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Parties to meet and confer on the above mentioned issues.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Vacate Fraudulently Obtained Court Orders, And For Related Relief
Off Calendar Sep-11-2017 Continued To Oct-23-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Confirm Title
Off Calendar Apr-17-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David King for Jerry Ku (Petitioner).
Thomas Huster for other Beneficiaries.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. King having purchased the property with his two Brothers, the property having been purchased by the Parents, whether Mr. King lied about not having any siblings, Mr. King not understanding the meaning of the terms, altering of the property not being explained by former Counsel when doing Estate planning, the intent of the three Brothers for the property to go to the last surviving Brother, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court denied the Petition to Confirm Title. Mr. Huster is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Confirm Title
